steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content:
The passage sets up a contrast with the word “but” after stating the proposal, indicating that the data will show the opposite.
The key signal is the phrase “but data … show that this is not the case; for example,” which tells us we need a counterexample.

steps.title: Match the counterexample to the data
steps.content:
Choice A notes that HEA 7 and HEA 95 have the same cobalt proportion; if the table shows their fracture toughness differs, this directly contradicts the idea that toughness rises with cobalt content.
The other choices either describe differing cobalt proportions that could still support the proposal or make claims not borne out by the table.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
